page: Microsoft Azure F4ams_v6 is a memory-intensive virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The CPU configuration allocates 1.0 thread per core and includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core capabilities. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance ranks in the top tier, and the Geekbench single-core score falls in the strong category. Multi-core performance, such as stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core, sits in the average tier. Memory bandwidth tests, including uncached read and write operations, also perform within the average range. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression and text processing benchmarks demonstrate strong performance, while LLM inference for text generation on a small 135M model is also strong. However, prompt processing on a medium 7B model is relatively weak, landing in the bottom tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the F4ams_v6 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio without the added cost of bundled local NVMe storage, making it a resource-dense option for memory-bound workloads that rely on external Premium Storage. The dedicated AMD EPYC cores provide predictable compute performance. This server is well-suited for memory-intensive applications, database caching layers, text processing pipelines, and lightweight single-threaded processing tasks.
description: The Microsoft Azure F4ams_v6 is a memory-intensive virtual machine configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 4 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via Microsoft Hyper-V, it features 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs but is Premium Storage capable. Performance benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core CPU execution and strong single-threaded decompression and text processing capabilities, while multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics remain average. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy operations that do not require local NVMe drives. It is best suited for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and single-threaded processing tasks.
